paragraphs: You have an Egyptian toe form with medium-width forefoot and a narrow heel. A mixed profile; forefoot and heel require different fits.

Beyond the obvious: Your arch is rather short, meaning your toes are relatively long. Especially when considering aggressive shoes look for sufficient height in the toe box to let your toes curl up. Additionally your instep is particularly low, so an adjustable closure is preferable to avoid dead space. Ideally double velcro or laces, rather avoid pure slippers. Your deep heel projects further back than most, so a deeper, more sculpted heel cup fits naturally. Your big toe drifts slightly inwards (could be a mild hallux valgus); we avoid very asymmetric lasts and prefer moderately asymmetric or wider-tipped designs that help the big toe stay in contact with the shoe tip.
interpretation.title: Your Foot Shape

paragraphs: You wear your Scarpa shoes in sizes EU 39.5 to 40, ranging from at street size to half a size down from your street size of 40. The typical downsize for Scarpa is 1.5 sizes down; the per-shoe diagnoses below break down each pair.

Your Scarpa Instinct VSR LV's toes feel squeezed while the other shoes fit on the toes. Your Scarpa Instinct VSR LV is built on a Greek toe form while you have Egyptian toes. The mismatch is the most likely cause; look for shoes with a Egyptian-compatible toe box.
interpretation.title: What Your Current Shoe Fit Tells Us

paragraphs: Based on your scan and current shoe fit, we target shoes with Egyptian toe form, narrow forefoot width (adjusted from normal scan result based on your current shoe fit), and narrow heel width.

Given your preference for sport climbing both indoors and outdoors and a balanced fit, we prioritize balanced-stiffness velcros or lace-ups with slight downturn and no asymmetry (shifted lower to accommodate your mild hallux valgus).
The softer, stiffer, and budget tiers below offer alternative options around this target.
interpretation.title: What to Look For

recommendations.description: Velcro with Egyptian toe form, narrow fit throughout, low forefoot volume. 4.2mm TRAX SAS rubber with a full, medium-soft midsole, resulting in a balanced-stiffness shoe with slight downturn and slight asymmetry.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Tradeoff: Slightly more asymmetric than we target.

recommendations.description: Velcro with Egyptian toe form, narrow fit throughout, low forefoot volume. 3.5mm RH rubber rubber with a full, medium midsole, resulting in a balanced-stiffness shoe with flat profile and symmetric profile.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Tradeoff: Slightly less downturned than we target.
